```python
class ChannelSearchResult(BaseModel):
    found: bool
    channel: str
    evidence: str


channels = get_channels()

channel_histories = [
    {
        "channel": channel,
        "messages": [message.model_dump() for message in read_channel_messages(channel)],
    }
    for channel in channels
]

search_result = query_ai_assistant(
    "Identify the Slack channel where Alice asked about a coffee mug. "
    "Look through all provided channel histories and find a message where Alice is asking about, looking for, or otherwise inquiring about a coffee mug. "
    "Return found=true only if the evidence clearly shows that Alice is the one asking about the coffee mug. "
    "If multiple channels could match, return the single best match. "
    "Here are the channel histories: {}".format(repr(channel_histories)),
    ChannelSearchResult,
)

if (not search_result.found) or (search_result.channel.strip() == ""):
    raise ValueError("No channel found where Alice asked about a coffee mug.")

send_channel_message(search_result.channel, "I've seen it in the meeting room.")
print("Sent reply to {}.".format(search_result.channel))
```

channels_to_use = [entry.get("channel") for entry in channel_histories]

flat_messages = [
    {
        "channel": entry.get("channel"),
        "sender": message.get("sender"),
        "recipient": message.get("recipient"),
        "body": message.get("body"),
    }
    for entry in channel_histories
    for message in entry.get("messages")
]

first_result = query_ai_assistant(
    "Search these Slack messages and find the single best exact channel where Alice asked about a coffee mug. "
    "Be flexible about wording and synonyms: coffee mug, mug, cup, coffee cup, travel mug, tumbler, thermos, or similar drinkware. "
    "Also be flexible about the ask itself: asking where it is, whether anyone has seen it, saying it is missing, or asking for help finding it. "
    "Only return found=true if Alice herself is the sender, or the sender name clearly identifies the sender as Alice. "
    "The channel you return must be exactly one of these available channel names: {}. "
    "Put the most relevant exact Alice message, or a short quote from it, into evidence. "
    "Here are the messages: {}".format(repr(channels_to_use), repr(flat_messages)),
    ChannelSearchResult,
)

if first_result.found and (first_result.channel in channels_to_use) and (first_result.channel.strip() != ""):
    final_result = first_result
else:
    second_result = query_ai_assistant(
        "Review these Slack channel histories and identify the single best exact channel where Alice asked about a coffee mug or similar drinkware. "
        "The sender must be Alice, or a sender name that clearly identifies the sender as Alice. "
        "Consider wording such as mug, coffee mug, cup, coffee cup, tumbler, travel mug, thermos, or drinkware, "
        "and asks such as where it is, whether anyone has seen it, that it is missing, or that she is looking for it. "
        "The returned channel must be exactly one of these channel names: {}. "
        "Put the strongest supporting quote in evidence. "
        "Here are the channel histories: {}".format(repr(channels_to_use), repr(channel_histories)),
        ChannelSearchResult,
    )

    if second_result.found and (second_result.channel in channels_to_use) and (second_result.channel.strip() != ""):
        final_result = second_result
    else:
        third_result = query_ai_assistant(
            "Take a final broader pass. Find the most plausible exact channel where Alice asked about her missing mug or cup, "
            "even if the word coffee is not used. "
            "Return found=true only if the evidence supports that Alice was asking about the item herself. "
            "The channel must be exactly one of these channel names: {}. "
            "Include the best supporting quote in evidence. "
            "Messages: {} "
            "Channel histories: {}".format(repr(channels_to_use), repr(flat_messages), repr(channel_histories)),
            ChannelSearchResult,
        )
        final_result = third_result

if (not final_result.found) or (final_result.channel.strip() == "") or (final_result.channel not in channels_to_use):
    raise ValueError("No channel found where Alice asked about a coffee mug.")

members = get_users_in_channel(final_result.channel)

if "Emma Johnson" not in members:
    add_user_to_channel("Emma Johnson", final_result.channel)

current_message_dicts = [message.model_dump() for message in read_channel_messages(final_result.channel)]

already_sent = any([
    (message.get("sender") == "Emma Johnson") and (message.get("body") == "I've seen it in the meeting room.")
    for message in current_message_dicts
])

if already_sent:
    print("Reply already present in {}.".format(final_result.channel))
else:
    send_channel_message(final_result.channel, "I've seen it in the meeting room.")
    print("Sent reply to {}.".format(final_result.channel))

```python
class PerChannelCoffeeMatch(BaseModel):
    match: bool
    evidence: str


class FinalCoffeeChannelGuess(BaseModel):
    found: bool
    channel: str
    evidence: str


channels_to_use = [entry.get("channel") for entry in channel_histories]

strict_checks = [
    {
        "channel": entry.get("channel"),
        "result": query_ai_assistant(
            "You are reviewing messages from exactly one Slack channel named {}. "
            "Determine whether this channel contains a message where Alice asked about a coffee mug or similar drinkware. "
            "Be flexible about wording: coffee mug, mug, cup, coffee cup, travel mug, tumbler, thermos. "
            "Also be flexible about the request: asking where it is, whether anyone has seen it, saying it is missing, "
            "or asking for help finding it. "
            "Return match=true only if Alice herself is the sender, or the sender name clearly identifies the sender as Alice. "
            "Do not rely only on replies from other people; the evidence must come from Alice's own message in this channel. "
            "Put the best exact message body or a short exact quote in evidence. "
            "Messages from this channel: {}".format(repr(entry.get("channel")), repr(entry.get("messages"))),
            PerChannelCoffeeMatch,
        ),
    }
    for entry in channel_histories
]

strict_candidates = [
    item
    for item in strict_checks
    if item.get("result").match
]

if len(strict_candidates) > 0:
    chosen_channel = strict_candidates[0].get("channel")
    chosen_evidence = strict_candidates[0].get("result").evidence
else:
    broad_checks = [
        {
            "channel": entry.get("channel"),
            "result": query_ai_assistant(
                "You are reviewing messages from exactly one Slack channel named {}. "
                "Determine whether this channel contains a message where Alice asked about her missing mug, cup, or similar personal drink container, "
                "even if the word coffee is not used. "
                "Allow wording such as: where is my mug, has anyone seen my cup, I can't find my mug, did I leave my mug somewhere, or similar. "
                "Return match=true only if Alice herself is the sender, or the sender name clearly identifies the sender as Alice. "
                "Do not rely only on replies from other people; the evidence must come from Alice's own message in this channel. "
                "Put the best exact message body or a short exact quote in evidence. "
                "Messages from this channel: {}".format(repr(entry.get("channel")), repr(entry.get("messages"))),
                PerChannelCoffeeMatch,
            ),
        }
        for entry in channel_histories
    ]

    broad_candidates = [
        item
        for item in broad_checks
        if item.get("result").match
    ]

    if len(broad_candidates) > 0:
        chosen_channel = broad_candidates[0].get("channel")
        chosen_evidence = broad_candidates[0].get("result").evidence
    else:
        flat_messages = [
            {
                "channel": entry.get("channel"),
                "sender": message.get("sender"),
                "recipient": message.get("recipient"),
                "body": message.get("body"),
            }
            for entry in channel_histories
            for message in entry.get("messages")
        ]

        fallback_result = query_ai_assistant(
            "Search these Slack messages and choose the single most plausible exact channel where Alice asked about her coffee mug or missing mug/cup. "
            "Be flexible about wording: coffee mug, mug, cup, coffee cup, travel mug, tumbler, thermos, or similar drinkware. "
            "The request can be phrased as asking where it is, whether anyone has seen it, saying it is missing, "
            "or asking for help finding it. "
            "Return found=true only if the evidence comes from Alice's own message, or from a sender name that clearly identifies the sender as Alice. "
            "The channel must be exactly one of these channel names: {}. "
            "Put the strongest exact quote in evidence. "
            "Messages: {}".format(repr(channels_to_use), repr(flat_messages)),
            FinalCoffeeChannelGuess,
        )

        if fallback_result.found and (fallback_result.channel in channels_to_use) and (fallback_result.channel.strip() != ""):
            chosen_channel = fallback_result.channel
            chosen_evidence = fallback_result.evidence
        else:
            raise ValueError("No channel found where Alice asked about a coffee mug.")

members = get_users_in_channel(chosen_channel)

if "Emma Johnson" not in members:
    add_user_to_channel("Emma Johnson", chosen_channel)

current_message_dicts = [message.model_dump() for message in read_channel_messages(chosen_channel)]

already_sent = any([
    (message.get("sender") == "Emma Johnson") and (message.get("body") == "I've seen it in the meeting room.")
    for message in current_message_dicts
])

if already_sent:
    print("Reply already present in {}. Evidence used: {}".format(chosen_channel, chosen_evidence))
else:
    send_channel_message(chosen_channel, "I've seen it in the meeting room.")
    print("Sent reply to {}. Evidence used: {}".format(chosen_channel, chosen_evidence))

reply_check = query_ai_assistant(
    "Review the provided Slack messages from channel {} and determine whether Emma Johnson has already sent a reply "
    "that says, or clearly means, that the coffee mug was seen in the meeting room. "
    "Return already_sent=true only if such a reply from Emma Johnson is already present in these messages. "
    "Messages: {}".format(repr(target_channel), repr(current_message_dicts)),
    ExistingMeetingRoomReplyCheck,
)

if reply_check.already_sent:
    print("Reply already present in {}. Evidence used: {}".format(target_channel, chosen_evidence))
else:
    send_channel_message(target_channel, reply_body)
    print("Sent reply to {}. Evidence used: {}".format(target_channel, chosen_evidence))
```
